summary: Excellent audience recording (NAK) of a summer‑90 stadium show, highlighted by a scorching Eyes > Estimated > Terrapin trio and soaring Let It Grow.
review: The tape captures an electrifying 1990 stadium performance with the second set often called “one of the best” – the Eyes > Estimated > Terrapin sequence, Let It Grow, Lovelight and a heartfelt We Bid You Goodnight stand out. The NAK‑based audience recording is praised as crisp, full‑range and well‑mixed, making the show easy to enjoy despite its massive venue. Overall fans rave about Jerry’s inspired playing, Brent’s keyboards and the festive crowd atmosphere.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews are 4‑5 stars, uniformly praising both the musical performance and the audio quality; recording is described as excellent and the show is repeatedly called a top‑tier summer ’90 concert.

recording_quality.technical_notes: NAK shotgun mics deliver focused, full‑range sound; reviewers call it crisp, clear and well‑balanced with no notable dropouts or noise issues.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as solid and well‑played; second set is the highlight, especially the extended trio jam and a string of tightly performed classics that kept the crowd energized.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for superb guitar tone, vocal strength and an inspired slide on "Teach Your Children"; called one of his best nights.
band_member_comments.Phil: Tom Thumb Blues singled out as a highlight of Phil’s contribution.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir noted for strong rhythm work and a memorable finish to Lovelight on one knee.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboards) lauded as the last Brent show, with fans calling his playing “great” and missing him afterward.
